skill.name: global-tech-stack
skill.description: Apply project technology standards when choosing frameworks, dependencies, databases, testing tools, build tools, deployment settings, authentication, and third-party services.
skill.summary: Keep implementation choices aligned with the project-approved technology stack.
skill.author: EIS-ITS

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported high-severity path traversal, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found no executable code, commands, network access, credential handling, or prompt injection. The only confirmed issue is a fixed relative Markdown link that leaves the skill directory, which is a low-risk packaging and boundary concern.

low_findings.title: Fixed Relative Path Traversal Reference
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 29 links to ../../../agent-os/standards/global/tech-stack.md. This leaves the skill directory, but it is a fixed Markdown reference with no execution, variable input, or data transfer.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The traversal sequence is directly present on line 29. Confidence is high that it is not an exploit because the file contains only prose and a static link.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography on prose lines in SKILL.md. No cryptographic algorithm, encryption API, import, script, or command appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are descriptive text only. There is no evidence of DES, MD5, SHA1, or any cryptographic operation in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alert Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 3 describes project technology choices and tool categories. It does not instruct the agent to enumerate the host system, collect environment data, or run discovery commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is skill metadata describing when to use the skill. No reconnaissance intent or operational instruction is present at that location.

content.user_title: Align Projects With Approved Tech Stacks
content.value_statement: Technology decisions drift when teams add tools without checking project standards. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to follow the approved stack.
actual_capabilities: Guides framework and library choices for new project features.
Helps evaluate dependency additions against existing technology standards.
Supports framework-specific work for Rails, Django, Next.js, Express, and similar stacks.
Prompts consistent handling of databases, ORMs, testing tools, build tools, and deployment settings.
Supports authentication, authorization, and third-party service integration decisions.
Helps document technology choices for onboarding and project maintenance.
limitations: The referenced detailed tech stack document is not included in this package.
It does not run automated checks or enforce dependency policies by itself.
It depends on repository context to identify the actual approved technologies.
It provides workflow guidance, not complete stack rules inside SKILL.md.

use_cases.title: Choose a Library for a Feature
use_cases.description: Compare a proposed library with the project stack before adding it to the codebase.

use_cases.title: Review Architecture Consistency
use_cases.description: Check whether a design or refactor follows established frameworks, databases, and service choices.

use_cases.title: Explain Stack Decisions During Onboarding
use_cases.description: Summarize the project technology choices and when each tool should be used.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Dependency Choice
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review before installing a package.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review whether adding [dependency] fits this project tech stack and current conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Feature With Existing Tools
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design [feature] with the frameworks, database tools, tests, and build setup already used here.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Proposed Architecture Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to evaluate this architecture proposal for stack alignment, dependency conflicts, and deployment impact: [proposal].

prompt_templates.title: Create a Stack Migration Recommendation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to assess whether replacing [current tool] with [new tool] is justified for this project.

output_examples.input: Should we add a second ORM for a reporting feature?
output: Recommendation: avoid adding a second ORM unless the current ORM cannot meet the reporting requirement.
Reasoning: another ORM increases model duplication, migration complexity, and testing surface.
Next step: verify whether the existing ORM supports read replicas, raw queries, or reporting views.

output_examples.input: Plan authentication for a new admin area.
output: Start with the project-approved authentication system and reuse its session, role, and audit patterns.
Document any missing authorization requirement before proposing a new package or service.
Add tests that cover login, role checks, denied access, and session expiry.

output_examples.input: Explain the stack to a new developer.
output_examples.output: The response lists the approved frameworks, data tools, test tools, build process, deployment path, and where each should be used.

best_practices: Provide the repository context and any existing stack documentation before asking for recommendations.
Ask for tradeoffs when a new dependency or service is being considered.
Use the skill before implementation, dependency installation, or architecture review.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to justify a new tool without checking existing project standards.
Do not expect it to enforce policies without repository files or CI rules.
Do not treat its guidance as a replacement for security or compliance review.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps keep technology choices consistent across frameworks, dependencies, databases, tests, build tools, and services.

faq.question: Does it install or run tools?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill is instruction-only and does not contain scripts or executable commands.

faq.question: Can it choose a new framework?
faq.answer: It can help evaluate a framework against project standards, but humans should approve major stack changes.

faq.question: Does it include the detailed stack rules?
faq.answer: No. It points to an external relative document that is not included in this package.

faq.question: Which assistants can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Manual review found only a low-risk fixed relative path reference and no malicious behavior.
